title: wendlandia tinctoria subsp. tinctoria
intro: Wendlandia tinctoria subsp. tinctoria, a plant species belonging to the family Rubiaceae, is commonly known as the 'Indian Turmeric' plant. The leaves and roots of this plant possess medicinal properties, containing compounds such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Wendlandia tinctoria subsp. tinctoria include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Traditionally, the leaves and roots of this plant are used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
